proj.note: Illustrative scenarios anchored at ~HK$40 (22 Jun 2026), built from the bear/base/bull DD logic. Returns are price-multiple based and ignore HK$/CHF FX moves. Probabilities and bands are subjective estimates — single-stock biotech can lose its entire value. Not financial advice.

px.note: USD ≈ HKD ÷ 7.80 (HK$ is USD-pegged). Verify live — intraday range was HK$36.1–44.5 on the SK-deal spike. The sparkline below works in-app; the full interactive TradingView chart renders in the online/hosted version.

rationale: Thesis intact: lead asset rentosertib (IPF) on track for oral Phase 3 start in H2 2026 — the defining catalyst.
Deal momentum strong: SK Biopharmaceuticals (up to US$2.5bn, 22 Jun) and Servier (US$888m, Jan) validate the platform — though both are milestone-heavy with small upfronts.
Near-term overhang: all major 6-month lock-ups expire ~29–30 Jun 2026; watch for VC/cornerstone selling.
Valuation still narrative-driven — stock round-tripped HK$35 → HK$81 → ~HK$33. Size small, stage entries.
note: Direction is re-assessed on every refresh. Recommended max position: CHF 6,000–8,000 (≤5% of portfolio). Analysis, not personalised financial advice — verify live price/FX before trading.

catalysts.d: Cornerstone & pre-IPO holders free to sell — potential supply overhang.
Pivotal trial for the lead AI-designed asset; the single biggest value driver.
China IND cleared; world-first AI drug in a direct-to-lung study.
Gut-restricted PHD1/2 inhibitor; BETHESDA trial dosing underway.
Brain-penetrant inhibitor (Hygtia collab); first-in-human dosing done 17 Jun 2026.
ISM6331 (pan-TEAD) & ISM3412 (MAT2A) global trials — solid-tumor data.
Revenue recovery, cash burn vs ~US$393m, updated pipeline guidance.
Guidance: 40–50 preclinical candidates over 2–3 yrs; partner milestones.

news.hl: Up to US$2.5bn AI drug-discovery deal with SK Biopharmaceuticals (neuroimmune/CNS)
news.summary: Announced at the BIO 2026 convention: Insilico and Korea's SK Biopharmaceuticals will discover AI-enabled candidates for neuroimmune/CNS disorders. Total potential value exceeds US$2.5bn (development, regulatory & commercial milestones + single-digit royalties); Insilico gets up to US$18m upfront/near-term. Exact release time not disclosed.
km: Largest headline deal value to date, but heavily back-loaded (only ~US$18m upfront)
SK leads late-stage development & commercialization; Insilico does discovery

news.hl: First-in-human dosing of NLRP3 inhibitor ISM8969 (Hygtia collab)
news.summary: First patient dosed in the Phase 1 (Australia; SAD + MAD) study of ISM8969, an oral, brain-penetrant NLRP3 inflammasome inhibitor for neuroinflammation/CNS disorders incl. Parkinson's. ~100 participants planned. Insilico eligible for up to US$66m in upfront + milestones; 50/50 global rights with Hygtia.
km: First clinical milestone in the Hygtia partnership
Brain-penetrant NLRP3 is a differentiated, high-interest mechanism
13 of 31 nominated candidates now have IND approval/clearance

news.hl: Rentosertib inhalation solution receives China IND clearance
news.summary: China's CDE cleared an IND for an inhaled formulation of rentosertib — described as the world's first AI-designed candidate to enter a direct-to-lung clinical study, running in parallel with the oral IPF program.
km: Second shot on goal for the lead IPF asset
Inhaled delivery could improve lung exposure / safety
Reinforces rentosertib as the franchise driver

news.hl: 3 abstracts on ISM5411 (IBD) at Crohn's & Colitis Congress 2026
news.summary: Insilico presented Phase 1 (healthy-volunteer) and preclinical safety/efficacy data for ISM5411, a gut-restricted PHD1/2 inhibitor for inflammatory bowel disease, ahead of its Phase 2a BETHESDA trial. Exact presentation date/time per congress agenda.
km: Builds the data package for the IBD program
Gut-restricted design targets efficacy with low systemic exposure

news.hl: US$888m multi-year oncology collaboration with Servier
news.summary: Insilico and France's Servier agreed a multi-year oncology drug-discovery collaboration worth up to US$888m in potential milestones plus royalties, announced from Cambridge, MA.
km: One of Insilico's largest deals; validates the discovery engine
Diversifies revenue toward recurring fees + milestones
Adds a blue-chip European pharma partner

news.hl: Lists on HKEX — 2025's largest Hong Kong biotech IPO
news.summary: Insilico listed on the HKEX Main Board (03696) at HK$24.05, raising ~US$293m in 2025's largest HK biotech IPO; the retail tranche was oversubscribed ~1,427x. Dealing commenced at the HK market open on 30 Dec 2025.
km: Resolves the prior private-market access barrier
First AI-biotech listed under HKEX Chapter 18A

lockups.basis: Voting pact, not a sale lock

lockupNote: Source: HKEX prospectus (18 Dec 2025). Principal lock-ups run ~6 months (180 days) from the 30 Dec 2025 listing → expiring ~29–30 Jun 2026.
structNote: No controlling shareholder: the largest holder (founder Zhavoronkov) controls only ~8.37% of votes, so HKEX's 12-month controlling-shareholder lock-up does not apply. Total shares include 383,418,500 ordinary shares from preferred conversion at listing. Source: HKEX prospectus.
holderNote: Subjective estimates of the probability each holder trims/exits in the months after lock-up expiry — NOT data or guidance. Logic: founders/strategics (Lilly) least likely to sell (signaling, partnership, voting control); VCs/asset managers most likely (return capital to LPs, mandate rebalancing). Re-assessed each refresh against actual HKEXnews disclosures of interest.
